title: fagonia californica subsp. pachyacantha
intro: Fagonia californica subsp. pachyacantha, commonly known as California bushmat or desert fagonia, is a shrubby plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Fagonia californica subsp. pachyacantha include reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional folk remedies to treat various health conditions.
